headline: Elderly Care Facilities Serve Only 6% of Seniors Needing Assistance
context: Malaysia has 340 registered elderly care homes but demand from aging population far exceeds available capacity.

cards.big: Only 6% of 420,000 seniors needing care assistance have access to formal services
cards.sub: Malaysia will become an aged nation by 2030 with 15% population over 60

cards.big: 340 registered care homes provide 18,000 beds for a growing elderly population of 3.8 million
cards.sub: Unregistered homes estimated at double the registered number with no quality oversight

cards.big: Average elderly care home costs RM2,500 monthly exceeding most retiree pension incomes
cards.sub: EPF average balance at retirement is only RM58,000 lasting 3 years of care costs

cards.big: Japan's Long-Term Care Insurance introduced in 2000 now covers 6.8 million elderly citizens
cards.sub: Mandatory social insurance model ensures universal access to aged care services

cards.big: A society that neglects its elderly is one that has forgotten its own future
Should Malaysia introduce mandatory long-term care insurance for all working adults
